tool_args.label: PAL Section 2897 General Rule
tool_args.raw_text: PAL Section 2897 provides that, as a general rule, any leasing-out or sale of real property can only be undertaken after public advertising for bids and for not less than fair market value.

payload.claim_text: PAL Section 2897 provides that, as a general rule, any leasing-out or sale of real property can only be undertaken after public advertising for bids and for not less than fair market value.
payload.subject: Real Property Disposition Guidelines
payload.statement: MTA Real Property Disposition Guidelines describe the PAL Section 2897 requirement that real property dispositions require public advertising for bids and fair market value

evidence_refs.source_quote: PAL Section 2897 provides that, as a general rule, any leasing-out or sale of real property can only be undertaken after public advertising for bids and for not less than fair market value.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: GCT Selection Criteria - Direct Economic Benefit
tool_args.raw_text: Selection Criterion A: Direct Economic Benefit to the MTA (Maximum 70 Points).

payload.claim_text: GCT Leasing Guidelines use Selection Criterion A: Direct Economic Benefit to the MTA (Maximum 70 Points) and Selection Criterion B: Indirect Benefit to the MTA (Maximum 30 Points).
payload.subject: GCT Leasing Guidelines
payload.statement: Selection Criterion A (Direct Economic Benefit) has max 70 points and Selection Criterion B (Indirect Benefit) has max 30 points for evaluating GCT commercial lease proposals

evidence_refs.source_quote: Selection Criterion A: Direct Economic Benefit to the MTA (Maximum 70 Points).

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Qualifying Surplus Property Threshold - $5,000
tool_args.raw_text: Disposals of Qualifying Surplus Property (i.e. Surplus Property with a Fair Market Value in excess of $5,000) shall comply with applicable provisions of PAL §§ 2895 - 2897

payload.claim_text: Disposals of Qualifying Surplus Property (i.e. Surplus Property with a Fair Market Value in excess of $5,000) shall comply with applicable provisions of PAL §§ 2895 - 2897
payload.subject: All-Agency Guidelines for the Disposal of Personal Property
payload.statement: Personal property valued over $5,000 is Qualifying Surplus Property subject to PAL §§ 2895-2897 requirements

evidence_refs.source_quote: Disposals of Qualifying Surplus Property (i.e. Surplus Property with a Fair Market Value in excess of $5,000) shall comply with applicable provisions of PAL §§ 2895 - 2897

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: LIRR MECH Employee Classification
tool_args.raw_text: Effective March 4, 2022, FRA added a third classification of employees to its federal drug/alcohol testing program: employees who, on behalf of a railroad, perform mechanical tests or inspections required by parts 215, 221, 229, 230, 232, or 238 of the CFR on railroad rolling equipment, or its components.

payload.claim_text: Effective March 4, 2022, FRA added a third classification of employees to its federal drug/alcohol testing program: employees who, on behalf of a railroad, perform mechanical tests or inspections required by parts 215, 221, 229, 230, 232, or 238 of the CFR on railroad rolling equipment
payload.subject: LIRR MED-005 Policy
payload.statement: LIRR MED-005 revised March 2022 to add Mechanical Employees (MECH) as a Regulated Service classification per 49 CFR 219

evidence_refs.source_quote: Effective March 4, 2022, FRA added a third classification of employees to its federal drug/alcohol testing program: employees who, on behalf of a railroad, perform mechanical tests or inspections

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: MNR Mechanical Employee Classification
tool_args.raw_text: Mechanical Employees – Applies to Employees who perform mechanical duties as defined in 49 CFR Part 219.5, which covers any Employee who performs mechanical tests or inspections required by parts 215, 221, 229, 230, 232, 238, or 299 of this chapter on railroad rolling equipment, or its components.

payload.claim_text: Mechanical Employees – Applies to Employees who perform mechanical duties as defined in 49 CFR Part 219.5, which covers any Employee who performs mechanical tests or inspections required by parts 215, 221, 229, 230, 232, 238, or 299 of this chapter on railroad rolling equipment, or its components.
payload.subject: MNR 21-012 Policy
payload.statement: MNR 21-012 revised March 2022 to add Mechanical Employees under Regulated Service classification

evidence_refs.source_quote: Mechanical Employees – Applies to Employees who perform mechanical duties as defined in 49 CFR Part 219.5

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.
